cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Leslie could have gone to the beach. So the problem says that Leslie went to the beach today, and we need to determine between which times that could have happened, given the different alibis from friends and the fact that the beach was closed after 10pm. Alright, let's break down the information given: 1. Leslie woke up at 5am. 2. Emily saw Leslie at school from 5am to 9am. 3. Anthony saw Leslie at the theater from 9am to 11am. 4. Thomas saw Leslie at the bakery from 11am to 4pm. 5. Ashley saw Leslie at the electronic store from 4pm to 5pm. 6. Susan saw Leslie walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 9pm to 10pm. 7. The beach was closed after 10pm. We need to find a time when Leslie wasn't doing any of these activities and before the beach was closed. Let's list the times when Leslie was occupied: - 5am to 9am: at school - 9am to 11am: at the theater - 11am to 4pm: at the bakery - 4pm to 5pm: at the electronic store - 9pm to 10pm: walking towards the Statue of Liberty So, between 5am and 9am, Leslie was at school. Then from 9am to 11am at the theater, then from 11am to 4pm at the bakery, then from 4pm to 5pm at the electronic store. There's a time gap between 5pm and 9pm when Leslie wasn't seen doing anything. Then from 9pm to 10pm, Leslie was walking towards the Statue of Liberty. And the beach was closed after 10pm. Therefore, the only time window when Leslie wasn't doing anything is between 5pm and 9pm. That's when Leslie could have gone to the beach, as long as they left before it closed at 10pm. Looking at the options: (A) 5pm to 9pm - This is the time window when Leslie wasn't seen doing anything, so this is possible. (B) 9am to 11am - Leslie was at the theater, so not possible. (C) 9pm to 10pm - Leslie was walking towards the Statue of Liberty, so
